rulings.Case Title: 360 6TH STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNI VS. J.H. NIELSEN, LLC et al

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 1. rulings.Case Title: NEIL ADAMS VS. CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 2. PLAINTIFF NEIL ADAMS'S DEMURRER TO CROSS- COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE TOLLING AGREEMENT APPLIES BASED ON THE FACTS ALLEGED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS ALLEGED VIABLE TRADE SECRET, BREACH OF CONTRACT AND UNFAIR COMPETITION CLAIMS. C.C.P. SECTION 2019.210 IS A DISCOVERY REQUIREMENT, NOT A BASIS FOR A DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL SIMME VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 18. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES ARE FACTUALLY DEVOID. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. TELLEZ V CORDOVA DOES NOT IMPOSE A DUTY TO WARN WHERE THE HAZARD ARISES FROM THE INCORPORATION OR ADDITION OF A PRODUCT MANUFACTURED BY A THIRD PARTY. THE REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. THE SOUGHT AFTER DISCOVERY IS NOT RELEVANT TO ESTABLISHING CRANE'S LIABILITY. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE ADAMS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 16. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff fails to present evidence sufficient to establish a prima facie case for plaintiff has not shown that the City is a joint employer under Vernon v. State of California (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 114. Assuming plaintiff did shift the burden of proof, defendant has established a nondiscriminatory reason for its decision. Although plaintiff presents evidence that he is a member of more than one protected class, he presents no evidence of disperate treatment. The statement attributed to Sgt. Tom is a stray comment as a matter of law. The conduct established by plaintiff does not support a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Finally, the decision to appoint plaintiff is a discretionary decision by the Chief of Police and it appears that Government Code section 818.4 grants the Chief, and in turn the City, immunity from suit when making that decision. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied for failure to provide the requisite showing under CCP 437c and for failing to explain why plaintiff has not had an adequate opportunity to obtain the evidence. =(302/PJM/301-AL)

rulings.Case Title: S.C.TANG INVESTMENTS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SUNG CHING TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Ofmotino To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT SUNG TANG'S Amended MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS ABOUT WHETHER A TRANSACTION IN HONG KONG MADE BETWEEN TWO CHINESE NATIONALS WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD. THE FACTS UPON WHICH PLAINTIFF RELIES DO NOT ARISE OUT OF, NOR HAVE A SUBSTANTIAL CONNECTION WITH THAT ACTIVITY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ENGELS et al VS. UTI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT U.T.I. UNITED STATES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE COURT MAY NOT RELY UPON A DECLARATION TO DECIDE A DEMURRER. C.C.P. SECTION 430.10(C) REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES BE ALIGNED IN BOTH CASES AND THE PARTIES ARE THE SAME IN BOTH CASES. DEFENDANT FAILS TO MEET EITHER REQUIREMENT. =(302/PJM/PB)
